toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to run the tests and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process.

For .NET tests, to collect crash dumps when the test host crashes, I can use the `dotnet test` command with the `--blame-crash` option. This is part of the `dotnet test --blame` family of options:

- `--blame-crash` - Collects a crash dump when the test host crashes
- `--blame-crash-dump-type` - Can be `full` or `mini`
- `--blame-crash-collect-always` - Collects a crash dump even on expected test host exit
  
Now I'll execute the command to run the tests with crash dump collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-e8b79bf72f6f428fb5cadf236d53795a && dotnet test --blame-crash --blame-crash-dump-type full 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command used:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash --blame-crash-dump-type full
```

- **`--blame-crash`** — monitors the test host process and collects a crash dump if it unexpectedly exits.
- **`--blame-crash-dump-type full`** — captures a full memory dump (use `mini` for smaller dumps).

No crashes occurred, so no dump was generated. If a test had crashed the host, the dump would be written to the `TestResults/` directory.
